title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Jock Itch
intro: Herbal teas for Jock itch are a natural alternative remedy that involves infusing various herbs in hot water to create a soothing and antifungal solution to combat the fungal infection. These herbal teas can treat jock itch due to their antifungal properties, which help to reduce the growth of the fungus and alleviate symptoms such as itching, redness, and inflammation. Examples of herbal teas used to treat jock itch include tea tree oil, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to combat fungal infections, as well as calendula, chamomile, and lavender teas, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the affected area. Other herbal teas such as oregano, thyme, and echinacea can also be used to treat jock itch due to their antifungal and immune-boosting properties.

preparations.herb_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including jock itch, due to their antimicrobial and antifungal properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat jock itch by reducing inflammation, relieving itching, and preventing the spread of fungal infections. The bioactive constituents of Melaleuca alternifolia, such as cymene and terpinene, have been shown to exhibit potent antifungal activity against fungi like Trichophyton mentagrophytes, which is responsible for jock itch. The benefits of using Melaleuca alternifolia teas to treat jock itch include reduced symptoms, improved skin health, and a natural alternative to conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Melaleuca alternifolia leaves.
Steep the leaves for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup.
Drink 1 cup, 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Melaleuca alternifolia teas, which contain tea tree oil, have antifungal activity against skin infections such as jock itch.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melaleuca alternifolia teas can be used to treat jock itch due to its antimicrobial properties, but possible side effects include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased heart rate. When using Melaleuca alternifolia teas to treat jock itch, take precautions such as performing a patch test before application, avoiding application on broken skin, and limiting the amount of tea used to prevent excessive absorption.
preparation_usage: Soak the affected area in a warm bath with 10 drops of Melaleuca alternifolia oil for 30 minutes.
Mix 10 drops of Melaleuca alternifolia oil with 1 cup of water and apply as a compress.
Drink 1 cup of Melaleuca alternifolia tea, 3 times a day, for 7 days.
Add 20 drops of Melaleuca alternifolia oil to 1 cup of warm water and use as a sitz bath.
Repeat the treatment for 7-14 days or until symptoms subside.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas, derived from the leaves of the eucalyptus tree, have been used to treat jock itch due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat jock itch by reducing the severity of symptoms such as itching, redness, and inflammation, allowing the skin to heal faster. The bioactive constituents of eucalyptus globulus teas, including cineole and eucalyptol, have been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against Candida species, a common cause of jock itch. The benefits of using eucalyptus globulus teas to treat jock itch include its natural origin, low risk of side effects, and ability to promote skin healing and reduce discomfort associated with the ailment.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Add 1/2 cup of boiling water to the leaves in a cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from jock itch symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be used to treat jock itch due to its antifungal properties, but potential side effects include allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, or redness, and gastrointestinal issues like nausea or stomach upset. Precautions to take when using Eucalyptus globulus teas to treat jock itch include avoiding ingestion in large quantities, as it may cause an imbalance of certain nutrients, and being cautious when applying topically to sensitive areas.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1 cup 3 times a day for 7-10 days.
Use 10 drops of Eucalyptus globulus essential oil in bath water.
Soak in bath for 15-20 minutes 2 times a week.
Repeat treatment for 2-3 weeks for best results.

preparation_amazon.title: Jovvily Eucalyptus Leaves - 1lb - Dried - Cut & Sifted - Herbal Tea

preparations.herb_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, also known as lemongrass tea, have been traditionally used to treat various skin issues, including jock itch. The antifungal properties of this herbal preparation help to treat jock itch by inhibiting the growth of fungal pathogens, such as Trichophyton and Candida, that cause the infection. The bioactive constituents of lemongrass tea, including citral and geranial, possess potent antifungal and antibacterial activities that contribute to its therapeutic effects. The benefits of using lemongrass tea to treat jock itch include its natural and non-invasive approach, reduced risk of side effects, and cost-effectiveness, making it a popular alternative remedy for this common skin condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves or 1/4 cup of dried leaves.
Boil 2 cups of water in a pot.
Add the Cymbopogon citratus leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool down.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from jock itch symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cymbopogon citratus teas can be used to treat jock itch, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues such as nausea and stomach upset. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include starting with a small dose, monitoring skin reactions closely, and avoiding use if you have sensitive skin or allergies to essential oils.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of Cymbopogon citratus tea in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1-2 cups of tea, 3-4 times a day, for 1-2 weeks.
Soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea and apply it to the affected area for 30 minutes.
Repeat the cloth application 2-3 times a day for 1-2 weeks.
Combine tea drinking and cloth application for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been traditionally used to treat jock itch, a fungal infection caused by the dermatophytes, by utilizing its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The anti-inflammatory properties of Aloe barbadensis teas help to soothe the affected skin, reducing redness and discomfort, while the antifungal properties inhibit the growth of the fungus, thereby treating the infection. The bioactive constituents present in Aloe barbadensis teas, such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan, contribute to its antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects, making it an effective remedy for treating jock itch. The benefits of using Aloe barbadensis teas to treat jock itch include its natural and non-invasive nature, which can be especially beneficial for individuals who experience skin irritation or allergic reactions to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of water and boil it.
Wash 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel with water, then pat dry.
Crush the dried aloe gel into fine pieces.
Mix 1 teaspoon of crushed aloe with 1 cup of the boiled water.
Let the mixture cool, then drink the aloe tea once a day.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be used to treat jock itch, but possible side effects include allergic reactions, stomach upset, and diarrhea due to its high content of anthraquinones, which can be irritating to some individuals. Precautions to take when using Aloe barbadensis teas for this purpose include starting with small doses, monitoring your body's response, and avoiding it if you have any history of gastrointestinal issues or are pregnant or breastfeeding.
preparation_usage: Soak a clean cloth in Aloe barbadensis tea, 1 cup warm water, 2 tablespoons tea.
Apply the cloth to the affected area for 30 minutes, 3 times a day.
Repeat the application for 7-10 days to see improvement.
Use Aloe barbadensis tea baths, 1 cup tea in 2 cups warm water, 2-3 times a week.
Combine tea baths with cloth applications for faster results and relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Calendula officinalis teas, derived from the marigold flower, possess antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that help to treat the jock itch ailment. The bioactive constituents, including triterpenoids, flavonoids, and carotenoids, exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities that contribute to the treatment of fungal infections. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, calendula officinalis teas can help alleviate the symptoms of jock itch, such as redness, itching, and discomfort. The benefits of using calendula officinalis teas to treat jock itch include reducing the severity and duration of the infection, preventing further irritation, and promoting overall skin health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Measure 2 tablespoons of Calendula officinalis flowers and put them in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour boiling water over the flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the flowers. Add 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) to the tea.
Drink 1/2 cup of the Calendula officinalis tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe jock itch.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "BMC veterinary research", Calendula officinalis teas may be beneficial for jock itch due to its broad-spectrum antibacterial and antifungal effects, which can help combat the fungal infections that cause jock itch.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Calendula officinalis teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating jock itch, but it may cause some side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or increased sensitivity to the sun, which can lead to sunburn or further skin damage. To minimize the risks, individuals should use Calendula officinalis teas in moderation, monitor their skin for any adverse reactions, and avoid exposing their treated skin to direct sunlight or extreme temperatures for an extended period after treatment.
preparation_usage: Wash the affected area with soap and water daily.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried Calendula officinalis in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed.
Soak a clean cloth in the tea and apply it to the affected area for 30 minutes, twice a day.
Continue treatment for 2-4 weeks for optimal results.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including jock itch, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Rosmarinus officinalis, such as carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, exhibit antifungal activity, which helps to combat the fungal infections that cause jock itch. By reducing inflammation and fighting off the fungal pathogens, Rosmarinus officinalis teas can help to alleviate the symptoms of jock itch, including itching, redness, and irritation. The benefits of using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat jock itch include natural and non-invasive treatment, reduced risk of side effects, and promotion of overall skin health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tbsp of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Add 1 tsp of honey to the tea if desired for taste.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate jock itch symptoms.
Repeat the process for 7-10 days to see noticeable improvements.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to treat jock itch, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications such as blood thinners, which can increase the risk of bleeding. To use Rosmarinus officinalis teas safely, it is essential to start with a small dose, monitor your skin for any adverse reactions, and avoid applying it to broken or sensitive skin areas.
preparation_usage: Wash affected area with soap and water before using tea.
Steep 2 tablespoons of Rosmarinus officinalis in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1 cup of tea, 2-3 times a day, for 7-10 days.
Soak a clean cloth in the tea and apply it to the affected area for 30 minutes, 2-3 times a day.
Repeat for 7-10 days and consult doctor if symptoms persist.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat various infections, including the jock itch ailment, also known as tinea cruris, which is caused by a fungal infection. The antifungal properties of Echinacea purpurea teas help to combat the fungal infection, reducing inflammation and promoting healing of the affected skin area. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, such as alkylamides, glycosides, and caffeic acid derivatives, have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities, making it an effective herbal remedy for treating jock itch. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat jock itch include reduced symptoms, improved skin health, and a natural alternative to conventional antifungal medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea roots and flowers.
Place the dried herbs in a tea infuser or a small piece of cheesecloth.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Pour the boiling water over the Echinacea purpurea herbs in the infuser or cheesecloth.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink as needed.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat fungal infections such as jock itch, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, skin irritation, and an increase in heart rate or blood pressure. To minimize potential risks, individuals should start with small doses, monitor their body's response, and avoid using Echinacea purpurea teas for extended periods, as well as avoid consuming them if they have any pre-existing medical conditions, such as high blood pressure or heart problems.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 3 times a day
Use 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea roots per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink
Continue for 7-10 days, or until symptoms subside
Combine with antifungal creams for faster relief

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat jock itch due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of thyme tea, including thymol and carvacrol, help to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause jock itch, thereby promoting healing and preventing further infection. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of thyme tea also help to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing itching and discomfort. By using thymus vulgaris teas to treat jock itch, individuals can benefit from a natural and effective remedy that not only alleviates symptoms but also prevents the recurrence of the condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh thyme leaves (Thymus vulgaris) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the thyme leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the thyme leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the thyme tea (optional) and mix well.
Drink 1/2 cup of the thyme tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe jock itch symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used as a potential treatment for jock itch, but possible side effects include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to sunlight, which may lead to photodermatitis. Precautions to take when using Thymus vulgaris teas to treat jock itch include avoiding prolonged exposure to direct sunlight and applying a gentle moisturizer to prevent dryness and irritation.
preparation_usage: Soak a clean cloth in 1 cup of warm Thymus vulgaris tea.
Gently apply the cloth to the affected area for 30 minutes.
Repeat this process 3-4 times a day for 7-10 days.
Use 2-3 tablespoons of Thymus vulgaris tea leaves per cup of water.
Drink 1 cup of Thymus vulgaris tea 2-3 times a day to boost immune system.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe vera teas have been gaining attention as a potential natural remedy to treat jock itch, a fungal infection caused by excessive moisture and sweat. The antifungal properties of aloe vera, particularly the bioactive constituents such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause jock itch. By consuming aloe vera tea, the body's immune system is boosted, allowing it to fight off the infection more effectively, and the anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera help to soothe the affected area, reducing itching and discomfort. The benefits of using aloe vera tea to treat jock itch include its natural and non-irritating properties, making it a safe and effective alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Cut 1 cup of aloe vera leaf into small pieces.
Soak the aloe pieces in 2 cups of hot water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a new container, discard the aloe pieces.
Add 2 tablespoons of honey and 1/2 teaspoon of apple cider vinegar to the liquid.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe jock itch.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe vera teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat jock itch, but its use may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high saponin content, which can be irritating to the digestive system. When using Aloe vera teas to treat jock itch, it is recommended to take precautions such as starting with small doses and gradually increasing the amount, avoiding consumption for extended periods, and being mindful of any allergic reactions or sensitivities to the plant.
preparation_usage: Soak a cotton ball with 1 cup of Aloe vera tea.
Apply the cotton ball to the affected area for 30 minutes.
Repeat the application 3 times a day for 1 week.
Use 1/2 cup of Aloe vera tea as a bath soak for 15 minutes.
Take 1 teaspoon of Aloe vera tea 2 times a day with water.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat jock itch, a fungal infection caused by the fungus Trichophyton rubrum. The antifungal properties of this herbal preparation help to inhibit the growth of the fungus, reducing the severity of the infection. The bioactive constituents, including linalool and linalyl acetate, possess antimicrobial properties that contribute to the antifungal activity, making it an effective treatment option. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat jock itch include reduced inflammation, accelerated wound healing, and a decrease in the risk of further infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Combine 1 cup of flowers with 1 quart of boiling water in a large pot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a large cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe jock itch symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat jock itch, but may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. Precautions include using diluted tea solutions, monitoring skin reactions, and avoiding use if you have sensitive skin or allergies, as the essential oils in Lavandula angustifolia may exacerbate the condition.
preparation_usage: Soak affected area in warm water with 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea
Apply 1 tablespoon of tea mixture to affected area 3 times a day
Use tea compress on affected area for 30 minutes, 3 times a day
Take 2 cups of Lavandula angustifolia tea orally 3 times a day
Repeat treatment for 7-10 days, then maintain with 1 cup of tea 2 times a week

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including the jock itch ailment. The antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to treat jock itch by reducing fungal growth and alleviating symptoms such as itching and redness. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, possess antifungal and antimicrobial properties that aid in combating fungal infections. The benefits of using ginger tea to treat jock itch include its natural and non-invasive approach, reduced risk of side effects, and potential long-term relief from symptoms.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh ginger root (Zingiber officinale) and chop it into small pieces.
Boil 1 cup of water in a saucepan and add 2 tablespoons of chopped ginger.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes to release the ginger's natural oils.
Strain the liquid into a separate cup and discard the ginger pieces.
Let the tea cool down, then apply it to the affected area using a cotton ball or swab.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat jock itch, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its high ginger content. Precautions should be taken when using Zingiber officinale teas to treat jock itch, including starting with small doses, avoiding use in people with sensitive stomachs or allergies, and monitoring for any adverse reactions to the treatment.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale tea in 1 cup of hot water.
Drink 2 cups of Zingiber officinale tea per day for 7 days.
Repeat treatment for 3 days if symptoms persist after initial treatment.
Soak affected areas in warm Zingiber officinale tea bath for 15 minutes.
Use a 1:1 ratio of Zingiber officinale tea and water as a compress for 30 minutes.

The antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation make it effective in treating jock itch by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the growth of fungal infections. The bioactive constituents, including silymarin and flavonoids, help to soothe and calm the affected skin, while also promoting the healing process. By using Silybum marianum teas, individuals can benefit from its ability to reduce symptoms and promote the recovery of jock itch, making it a natural and effective alternative to traditional treatments.

Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the dried Silybum marianum to the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from jock itch symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used as a treatment for jock itch, but its use may be associated with side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. The active compounds in Curcuma longa teas, such as curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, possess potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory activities that help to inhibit the growth of fungi and reduce inflammation in the affected area. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa teas, including polyphenols and terpenoids, contribute to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them an effective herbal remedy for treating jock itch. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat jock itch include reduced itching and inflammation, improved wound healing, and prevention of further fungal infections.

Boil the water in a pot and reduce heat to a simmer.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Curcuma longa root to the water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Drink 1 cup of the Curcuma longa tea 2-3 times a day to help treat jock itch.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM", Curcuma longa teas may be beneficial for jock itch due to its potential wound healing properties, however, there is no specific mention of jock itch in the study provided, but it may help promote skin health and wound healing, which could potentially aid in the recovery from jock itch.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be an effective alternative remedy for treating jock itch, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin or stomach conditions. To minimize potential side effects, it's recommended to start with a small dose, monitor your body's response, and be cautious when using Curcuma longa teas on open wounds or sensitive areas, as well as avoid consuming it in large quantities or for extended periods.

Apply the soaked cotton ball directly on the affected area.

Repeat the process 3 times a day for 7-10 days.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been explored as a potential herbal remedy to treat jock itch, a fungal infection caused by dermatophytes. The anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties of ginkgo biloba teas, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents such as flavonoids and terpenoids, help to reduce redness and itching associated with the ailment. The antifungal properties of ginkgo biloba teas, specifically the activity of bilobalide and quercetin, inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, thereby helping to treat jock itch. The benefits of using ginkgo biloba teas to treat jock itch include reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and a lower risk of recurrence, making it a potential natural remedy for this common fungal infection.

Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the tea infuser or muslin bag.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then remove the tea infuser or muslin bag.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can potentially treat jock itch, but may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, or increased risk of bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties. It is recommended to be cautious when using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat jock itch, as it may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and cause adverse effects when consumed in large quantities or combined with other herbal remedies.

preparations.herb_desc: Allium sativum teas, made from the extract of garlic, have been traditionally used to treat various skin infections, including jock itch. The antifungal properties of garlic help to combat the fungal infections that cause jock itch, while its antibacterial properties prevent the growth of bacteria that can lead to secondary infections. The bioactive constituents of garlic tea, such as allicin and diallyl disulfide, exhibit potent antimicrobial activity, which helps to reduce the severity and duration of jock itch symptoms. By consuming garlic tea, individuals can benefit from its antiseptic and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe and heal the affected skin, reducing the risk of recurring infections and promoting overall skin health.

Wash your hands and Allium sativum leaves with soap and water
Steep the Allium sativum leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes
Strain the tea and discard the leaves, add honey if desired for taste
Drink 1/2 cup of the Allium sativum tea 2-3 times a day for jock itch relief
preparations.preparation_study: According to Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology, Allium sativum teas, which is the scientific name for garlic, is primarily used for cardiovascular health and relief of cough, colds, and rhinitis, but there is no mention of its use for jock itch in the provided study.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Allium sativum teas can be a potential treatment option for jock itch, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or increased sensitivity to the sun, which can exacerbate the condition. When using Allium sativum teas to treat jock itch, it's essential to take precautions such as avoiding prolonged use, patch testing on a small area of skin, and being cautious not to apply the tea to broken skin to minimize potential risks.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been used traditionally to treat various ailments, including jock itch, due to their anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents, including quinine, quinidine, and alkaloids, in this herbal preparation help to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause jock itch, providing relief from itching and discomfort. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat jock itch include reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and a decrease in the severity of symptoms. Additionally, these teas may also help to prevent future infections by promoting a healthy balance of the skin's natural flora.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and add it to 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid with a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of apple cider vinegar and 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the liquid.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for 1-2 weeks to help alleviate jock itch symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat jock itch, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. Precautions when using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat jock itch include being cautious of interactions with other medications, as it may enhance the effects of blood thinners, and also being mindful of allergic reactions, such as hives or itching.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as stinging nettle tea, have been used to treat various health issues, including jock itch. The antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties of Urtica dioica teas help to treat jock itch by reducing redness and itching associated with the ailment. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica teas, such as flavonoids and saponins, possess antimicrobial properties that help to combat fungal infections that cause jock itch. The benefits of using Urtica dioica teas to treat jock itch include reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and the potential to prevent the recurrence of fungal infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Urtica dioica leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the dried Urtica dioica leaves to the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 2 cups per day as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to The Annals of pharmacotherapy, Urtica dioica teas have been reviewed in the context of treating BPH symptoms, but there is no direct information on their use for jock itch.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be used to treat jock itch, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, and allergic reactions like skin irritation and itching. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid using Urtica dioica teas in combination with other medications or herbal supplements that may interact with it.

preparations.herb_desc: Melaleuca cajuputi teas have been traditionally used to treat jock itch, a fungal infection caused by dermatophytes. The antifungal properties of this herbal preparation, which is rich in bioactive constituents such as terpenoids and flavonoids, help to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens and reduce inflammation. By applying a topical solution made from Melaleuca cajuputi teas to the affected area, individuals can help to soothe and heal the skin, reducing the symptoms of jock itch such as redness, itching, and discharge. The benefits of using Melaleuca cajuputi teas to treat jock itch include reduced risk of recurrence, faster healing time, and fewer side effects compared to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Melaleuca cajuputi leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Boil the water and then add the dried Melaleuca cajuputi leaves.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the leaves. Let the tea cool.
Drink 1 cup of the Melaleuca cajuputi tea, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe jock itch.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melaleuca cajuputi teas can potentially cause allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and diarrhea when used to treat jock itch, especially if consumed in large quantities or over an extended period. To minimize the risks, users should be cautious when ingesting Melaleuca cajuputi teas and avoid consuming them in combination with other medications or herbal remedies.

preparations.herb_desc: Origanum vulgare teas, derived from the leaves of the common oregano plant, have been traditionally used to treat jock itch, a fungal infection caused by Trichophyton species. The antifungal properties of Origanum vulgare teas, particularly carvacrol and thymol, help to combat the fungal growth and reduce inflammation in the affected area. The tea's antimicrobial properties also prevent the spread of the infection, promoting a speedy recovery and reducing the risk of recurrence. Regular consumption of Origanum vulgare teas has been found to provide relief from jock itch symptoms, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 2 tablespoons of dried Origanum vulgare leaves, and a tea infuser.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 1 minute.
Add the dried Origanum vulgare leaves to the tea infuser and place it in the pot with the water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser and tea leaves.
Let the tea cool down, then drink 1/2 cup of it 2-3 times a day to help soothe jock itch.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can be used to treat jock itch, but they may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or gastrointestinal upset in some individuals, particularly those who are sensitive to thymol or carvacrol, the main compounds found in the herb. Precautions should be taken when using Origanum vulgare teas to treat jock itch, including avoiding excessive consumption, using dilute solutions, and monitoring the body's reaction to the treatment to minimize potential side effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Saponaria officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat jock itch, a fungal infection caused by the presence of fungi such as Trichophyton and Epidermophyton. The antifungal properties of Saponaria officinalis teas help to treat this ailment by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells and reducing the severity of symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Saponaria officinalis teas, including saponins and alkaloids, contribute to its antifungal properties, helping to alleviate itching, redness, and inflammation associated with jock itch. The benefits of using Saponaria officinalis teas to treat jock itch include reduced risk of infection, faster recovery time, and minimal side effects, making it a popular natural remedy for this common fungal infection.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Saponaria officinalis leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the leaves and place them in a cup.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the cup with the Saponaria officinalis leaves.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, until jock itch symptoms subside.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Saponaria officinalis teas can be used to treat jock itch, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and diarrhea. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include avoiding its use if you have sensitive skin, and being cautious of interactions with other medications, as well as using it in moderation and monitoring your body's response.

preparation_study: According to the provided scientific study, Melaleuca alternifolia teas have been found to have beneficial effects on skin, including antifungal activity against skin infections, but there is no specific mention of the efficacy of Melaleuca alternifolia teas for jock itch, however, tea tree oil from Melaleuca alternifolia has been found to have antifungal, antiviral, antibacterial, and acaricidal activity against skin infections such as scabies.
intro_study: According to "The Australasian journal of dermatology", teas for jock itch, specifically those containing tea tree oil, may be able to reduce the symptomatology of tinea pedis, but they are not more effective than placebo in achieving a mycological cure.
